header.title: Apply for an Employer Identification Number (EIN)
additionalText: Use this assistance to apply for and obtain an Employee Identification Number (EIN)

instructions1.title: Legal Structure
additionalText: All fields marked with an asterisk (<Red>*</Red>) are required.

instructions2.title: What type of legal structure is applying for an EIN?
additionalText: If you still don't see your organization type, click on "View Additional Types including Non-Profit/Tax-Exempt Organizations" to view more types. Additional help may be found by reviewing <IRSLink2/>.

IRSLink2.text: all types of organizations and structures
IRSLink2.label: all types of organizations and structures

additionalText: Includes individuals who are in business for themselves and household employers.
Includes partnerships and joint ventures.
Includes S corporations, personal service corporations, real estate investment trusts (REIT), regulated investment conduits (RIC), and settlement funds.
A limited liability company (LLC) is a structure allowed by state statute and is formed by filing articles of organization with the state.
An estate is a legal entity created as a result of a person's death.
All types of trusts including conservatorships, custodianships, guardianships, irrevocable trusts, revocable trusts, and receiverships.

choices.text: View Additional Types, Including Tax-Exempt and Governmental Organizations
additionalText: If none of the above fit what you are establishing , there are several others to choose from.

instructions3.title: You have chosen Sole Proprietor
additionalText: Sole proprietor includes individuals who are in business for themselves, or household employers. Read the descriptions below and choose the type for which you are applying.

soleProprietorStructureInputControl.fieldName: Choose the type of legal structure

additionalText: A sole proprietorship is a business that has only one owner and is not incorporated or registered with the state as a limited liability company (LLC). A <IRSLink1/> can be a self-employed individual or an independent contractor. Sole proprietors (self-employed individuals) report all business income and expenses on their individual tax returns (<IRSLink2/>, U.S. Individual Income Tax Return, Schedule C, E, or F). A sole proprietor may or may not have employees.

You are a <IRSLink3/> if you have hired someone to do household work and <IRSLink4/>. Household employees include: babysitters, nannies, au pairs, cleaning people, housekeepers, maids, drivers, health aides, private nurses, caretakers, yard workers, and similar domestic workers.

inputErrorMessages.text: Sole Proprietor Type: Selection is required.

instructions3.title: You have chosen Partnerships
additionalText: Read the descriptions below and choose the type for which you are applying

partnershipStructureInputControl.fieldName: Choose the type of legal structure

additionalText: A <IRSLink1/> is a relationship existing between two or more persons or groups who join together to carry on a trade or business.  Each partner contributes money, property, labor, or skill, and expects to share in the profits and losses of the business.
A <IRSLink2/> is a partnership formed between two or more business entities. These businesses share risk or expertise on a specific project or group of projects.

instructions3.title: You have chosen Corporations
additionalText: Read the descriptions below and choose the type for which you are applying.

corpStructureInputControl.fieldName: Choose the type of legal structure

additionalText: A <IRSLink1/> is a person or group of people who establish a legal entity by filing articles of incorporation with the state's secretary of state granting it certain legal powers, rights, privileges, and liabilities.
The income of an <IRSLink2/> generally is taxed to the shareholders of the corporation rather than to the corporation itself. However, an S corporation may still owe tax on certain income.
A <IRSLink3/> involves services in the fields of health, law, engineering, architecture, accounting, actuarial science, performing arts, or consulting.
A <IRSLink4/> is an investment vehicle established for the benefit of a group of real estate investors.
A <IRSLink5/> is a regulated investment company that applies to any domestic corporation that meets certain criteria.
A <IRSLink6/> is established for the principal purpose of settling and paying claims against the electing taxpayer under Internal Revenue Code (IRC) Section 468B.

instructions3.title: Identify the type of Trust
additionalText: You must identify one <IRSLink1/> you are applying for. If you don't see your trust type, select "Trust (All Others)"

trustEntityInputControl.fieldName: Choose the type of legal structure

instructions3.title: Additional Types
additionalText: In order to obtain an EIN from the IRS, you must know what type of structure or organization you are setting up. If you need additional assistance, consult an accountant or other tax professional before applying for your EIN, or call the IRS at 1-800-829-4933.

additionalEntityInputControl.fieldName: Choose the type of legal structure

choices.text: Employer Plan (401K, Money Purchase Plan, etc.)

marriedInputControl.fieldName: Are the members husband and wife?

whichLlcInstructions.title: You are husband and wife residing in a community property state and the only two members of a Limited Liability Company (LLC)
additionalText: Under Revenue Procedure 2002-69, you have the option of treating the LLC as a <IRSLink1/> or as a <IRSLink2/>

whichLlcInputControl.fieldName: Please choose how you want to be classified

instructions3.title: Why is the {{entityName}} requesting an EIN?
additionalText: If your main reason for applying is not on the list, please select the option you feel is closest to your main reason. If more than one reason applies to you, choose the best or main reason.

corpTypeInstructions3.title: Why is the {{entityName}} requesting an EIN?
additionalText: If your main reason for applying is not on the list, please select the option you feel is closest to your main reason. If more than one reason applies to you, choose the best or main reason.

Note: If you were incorporated outside of the United States or U.S. territories, you cannot apply for an EIN online. Please call us at 267-941-1099 (this is not a toll free number). Please exit the application by clicking "Exit" above.

iraInstructions3.title: Why is the {{entityName}} requesting an EIN?
additionalText: Note: If you do not intend to file (U.S. Income Tax Return for Estates and Trusts) or (Exempt Organization Business Income Tax Return), you do not need an EIN. Please use your Social Security Number for all filing purposes and exit the application. Otherwise, click "Continue" to apply for an EIN.

reasonForApplyingInputControl.fieldName: Choose one reason that best describes why you are applying for an EIN

additionalText: If you are beginning a new business.
If you already have a business and need to hire employees.
If the reason for applying for the EIN is strictly to satisfy banking requirements or local law.
If you are changing the type of organization you currently operate, such as changing from a sole proprietor to a partnership, changing from a partnership to a corporation, etc.
If you are purchasing a business that is already in operation.

inputErrorMessages.text: Reason For Applying: Selection is required.

receivershipReasonInputControl.fieldName: Choose one reason that best describes why you are applying for an EIN

additionalText: If you already have a business and need to hire employees.
If the reason for applying for the EIN is strictly to satisfy banking requirements or local law.

inputErrorMessages.text: Reason For Applying: Selection is required.

homeownersReasonInputControl.fieldName: Choose one reason that best describes why you are applying for an EIN

additionalText: If you are beginning a new business.
If you already have a business and need to hire employees.
If the reason for applying for the EIN is strictly to satisfy banking requirements or local law.

inputErrorMessages.text: Reason For Applying: Selection is required.

typesOfLegalStructures.title: Types of Legal Structures
additionalText: <ul>
typesOfLegalStructures.ariaText: Types of Legal Structures help topic

memberHelp.title: Member
additionalText: The word "member" has several definitions:
1. An individual who belongs to an organization, association, firm, partnership, company, or corporation
• One of the persons who compose a social group (especially individuals who have joined and participate in a group organization)

• The owners of an LLC are called members and are similar in some respects to shareholders of a corporation. A member can be a natural person, a corporation, a partnership, or another legal association or entity

communityPropertyStateHelp.title: Community Property States
additionalText: Arizona, California, Idaho, Louisiana, Nevada, New Mexico, Texas, Washington, and Wisconsin.
